synthesis.summary: COHR surged 6.82% today but the only supplied news item concerns SpaceX's valuation and its impact on space sector rivals — unrelated to Coherent Corp's core business in optical components and photonics. No news in the provided set explains this move, which may reflect sector rotation, options flow, or AI/datacom infrastructure momentum not captured here.

Lennar reported a Q2 earnings miss, which directly accounts for the ~5% drop on double the normal volume. Despite an analyst noting lower incentives and positive consumer commentary as potential bright spots, the headline miss was sufficient to drive the selloff. The SpaceX news is unrelated to homebuilding and carries no explanatory weight here.

SpaceX began trading today, with its share price surging from $150 to $175, directly pressuring EchoStar/SATS as a competing satellite operator. The market re-rated SATS downward on fears that a newly public, well-capitalized SpaceX will intensify competitive pressure in the satellite communications space, explaining the -12% drop on nearly 6x normal volume.

SpaceX began trading publicly, with shares jumping from $150 to $175 on debut day. As a direct competitor in the satellite connectivity space, SpaceX's high-profile listing likely triggered rotation out of ASTS as investors shifted capital toward the newly public market leader, pressuring ASTS shares by over 14% on nearly double normal volume.

FLY dropped 17% today with no directly explaining news in the supplied set. The only high-impact item covers SpaceX's valuation jump and its impact on space rivals, which could pressure FLY if it operates in adjacent aerospace/space markets, but the magnitude of the decline and specific mechanism are not explained by this news alone. Relative volume is only modestly elevated at 1.2x, suggesting this may be driven by sector rotation, options activity, or company-specific news not captured here.

RDDT dropped 6.1% on near-average volume with no company-specific news in the feed to explain the move. The only high-impact item covers SpaceX's valuation jump, which has no direct bearing on Reddit's business. The decline likely reflects broader market rotation, sector-level selling, or options/flow dynamics not visible in the supplied data.

RDW dropped 9.3% over the session with no company-specific news in the supplied feed to explain it. The only available news item concerns SpaceX's private market trading debut, which could create competitive sentiment pressure on smaller space/defense firms like Redwire, but that's speculative — the magnitude and timing of this move aren't directly accounted for by that story. Below-average relative volume (0.76) suggests this isn't a high-conviction institutional sell-off, pointing more toward sector rotation or options flow.

SATL dropped ~10% with no company-specific news explaining the move. The only high-impact news in the feed is SpaceX beginning to trade publicly, which could be drawing capital away from smaller satellite/space-related names like SATL as investors rotate into the newly available SpaceX shares — but this is speculative and the magnitude of the drop isn't directly accounted for by that event.

VOYG dropped ~14.7% on no news directly related to the company. The only high-impact news in the feed concerns SpaceX's valuation jump and its effect on space-sector rivals, which has no plausible mechanism connecting to VOYG. Volume was near average (rel. volume 0.97), suggesting this is not a broad liquidity-driven event — the cause is likely options flow, a large block trade, or sector rotation not captured in the supplied news.

INTC surged 7.19% on elevated but not extreme volume, with no news in the supplied set that explains the move. The only high-impact item covers SpaceX's valuation jump and its effect on space-sector rivals, which has no direct relevance to Intel's business. The 5-day gain of 26.68% suggests a broader momentum or sector rotation story that isn't captured here.

SpaceX received a new private valuation of $175B, which appears to have triggered a sector rotation out of listed space names like RKLB as investors weighed the competitive implications of a better-capitalized rival. The -7.57% drop on 2.18x relative volume is consistent with a competitive overhang repricing rather than any company-specific negative catalyst at Rocket Lab.
